entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release (fka SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) has been shipped on January 14, 2026.

With every release update, our goal is to make your interaction with the system more intuitive, efficient, and intelligent. The 2602 release is a significant step forward, focusing on consistency, AI-powered assistance, enhanced personalization, and on-the-go mobility. Let's dive into the highlights in the area of SAP User Experience!

The new shell bar is here to stay

Remember the redesigned shell bar we introduced as an opt-in feature back in 2508? Based on positive feedback and our drive for a unified experience, it is now activated by default in the 2602 release.

The new shell bar provides:

  • An updated user menu: With a larger user image and more space between items, finding what you need is easier than ever. We've also moved "Settings" to the top and separated "Sign Out" for quicker access.

     

  • Redesigned notifications: A simplified, more pleasing design with easy sorting (by date or importance) and simple grouping (Today, Yesterday, etc.) helps you stay on top of your tasks without the clutter.

Important note for a smooth transition: As this is now the default, we strongly recommend you check any test automation or RPA scripts you have, as they may need to be adapted to the new user menu structure. For this release only, you can temporarily opt-out via the "Activate New Features" switch (feature `/UI2/SHELL_BAR_TOGGLE`), but please plan to adapt your systems, as the opt-out option will not be available from the 2608 release onwards.

Direct app launch in the SAP Fiori launchpad

We've heard you! Many of our experienced users coming from the SAP GUI world missed the ability to quickly launch an application using its transaction code. I'm delighted to announce that you can now launch apps directly from the SAP Fiori launchpad search bar using the app ID or transaction code.


For power users, this restores a familiar and efficient navigation pattern, lowering the barrier for those transitioning to SAP Fiori, as they can combine new UX principles with known shortcuts.

Enhancements to My Home

My Home continues to evolve as your central, personalized starting point. In 2602, we're giving you more control and enhancing communication:

  • Required reading for news: Administrators can now mark important news articles, such as new policies or critical process changes, as "required reading." Users must acknowledge these articles, which helps ensure compliance and consistent knowledge across the organization.

  • Greater layout control: You can now choose to hide the entire "News" or "Pages" sections on your My Home screen. This allows you to reduce clutter and tailor your homepage to display only the information and tools that are most relevant to your role, boosting your personal productivity.



An intelligent assistant for your daily tasks

Artificial intelligence is at the heart of the intelligent enterprise, and we're embedding it directly into your user experience.

The 2602 release distinguishes between base and premium AI features:

  • Base features (free of charge): After signing the acceptance of SAP AI terms for base AI-assisted features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you gain access to features like AI-assisted enterprise search, easy filter, and smart summarization.
  • Premium features (requires license): With the appropriate licenses (like SAP AI units and package Joule Premium for financal management), you can unlock advanced capabilities. In 2602, we are excited to announce the general availability (GA) of AI-assisted smart personalization of applications in My Home and AI-assisted error explanation.

Furthermore, we are continuing to beta test AI-assisted task automation, which allows users to create "smart helpers" to automate repetitive activities. This powerful tool keeps the user in the loop while dramatically improving efficiency. If you're interested in shaping the future of AI in our products, I encourage you to sign up for our beta testing program here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4086

Fast with SAP Fiori

We’re thrilled to announce the launch of our brand-new video series, "Fast with SAP Fiori"! The first three episodes are now live and ready to accelerate your SAP Fiori journey.

What’s coming:

  • Complete 10-episode series covering essential SAP Fiori concepts
  • Soon available on YouTube for easy access
  • Multi-language support including English, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian

Ready to dive in? Check out the first three episodes now, and stay tuned for the complete series launch!

  • Video 1 - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/ce7RMfU?rc=10&doi=SAP1286910 
  • Video 2 - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/Ef5HP5X?rc=10&doi=SAP1286912 
  • Video 3 - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/558Yrrv?rc=10&doi=SAP1286922 

Productivity on the go: SAP Fiori for mobile optimization

In today's flexible work environment, being productive on your smartphone is essential. That's why we've introduced SAP Fiori elements mobile phone optimization. This delivers a mobile-first experience by providing a compact, modern, and high-performance framework for key mobile scenarios. We've simplified navigation and reduced complexity by focusing on what's essential for users on the move, allowing you to get business done anywhere, anytime. Feel free to sign up for our beta testing program here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/4090

A single front door: Introducing SAP Start (SAP-managed)

Imagine a single, unified landing page for all your SAP Cloud ERP products. That is the vision for SAP Start, and with the 2602 release, we are introducing a new SAP-managed version.

This means you can activate it directly from SAP for Me with no additional implementation effort. In its initial release, SAP Start provides seamless, single sign-on access to SAP Cloud ERP, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Concur, and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. This is just the beginning, and we are eager for your feedback through our Early Adopter Care program to shape its future.

 

Your voice matters: Get involved!

Many of the features in this release, including the highly-requested direct app launch, originated as suggestions on the SAP Customer Influence Portal (CIP.) This is proof that your feedback directly shapes the future of our products.

I encourage you to explore these new features, see how they fit your business needs, and continue to share your insights with us through the SAP Community and the Customer Influence portal.

The 2602 release truly elevates the SAP User Experience, and we can't wait to see how you leverage these innovations to run your business better.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for SAP User Experience as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) Modernization Partner Demo Systems Reprovisioning
entry.content:
SAP is modernising all ECP Partner Demo Systems — moving from the older ERP HCM (ECC) stack to the powerful, future-ready S/4HANA HCM platform. This means faster performance, greater scalability, and a demo experience that truly reflects the innovation your customers expect.

What’s In It For You?

  • A brand-new, high-performing demo environment on S/4HANA HCM
  • Impress prospects with a modern platform that showcases ECP at its very best
  • Future-proof your demos with long-term SAP platform support
  • No disruption — your existing system stays live for a full 4 weeks while you transition

This is a reprovisioning, not a conversion — you’ll receive a fresh system with a new SID and URL, ready to go.

Your Transition Timeline at a Glance

PhaseWhat Happens
Request SubmittedSubmit via the SAP Partner Cloud Ops Portal
New System Goes LiveFresh S/4HANA HCM environment delivered
4-Week Overlap BeginsMigrate, configure, and validate — both systems run in parallel
Legacy System RetiredAutomatic decommissioning — your modern system takes centre stage

Here’s Your Fast-Track Checklist

Before you submit, make sure you’re set up for success:

  • Transports Ready — Workbench and Customizing transports prepared and S/4HANA compatible
  • Team Assigned — Functional and technical resources available to import, configure, and test
  • Plan Committed — All migration and validation activities completed within 1 month of go-live

How to Request Your New Demo System

  1. Log in to the SAP Partner Cloud Ops Portal using your partner credentials
  2. From the top navigation, select Support
  3. Locate and select ECP Partner Demo Reprovisioning
  4. Complete the request template in a Word document and attach it to the ticket
  5. Ensure your submission aligns with your data center-specific timelines

After Your New System Is Live

Once your new S/4HANA HCM demo system is provisioned, raise a support ticket to request:

  • Import of source system transports (Workbench, Customizing, and Add-ons where applicable)
  • Support for system migration and movement activities
  • Provide a full list of transports to be imported
  • Identify any network or integration considerations (e.g., SNC, interfaces, EC–ECP integrations)

Key Reminders

The legacy ERP HCM demo system will not be extended beyond the 4-week overlap period. Decommissioning is automatic — ensure all activities are completed on time.

Questions?

For any questions or clarifications, please reach out to the SAP ECP Modernization team at: SAP_SuccessFactors_ECP_Modernization@sap.com

We’re committed to making this transition as smooth as possible and look forward to supporting you through this modernization journey.

entry.title: Re: Configuration of the Trigger Event for VAT Declaratio...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I asked our internal chatbot for support and got the following response. Please check whether the procedure works for you:

To configure the triggering event for when supplier invoices become eligible for VAT declaration under the cash accounting regime, you can follow these steps:

  1. Configuration for Bank Transfers:

    Ensure that the invoice appears only after bank reconciliation is completed. This can be managed by setting up the bank reconciliation process to update the status of the invoice once the reconciliation is complete. You can use the ‘Manage Bank Statements’ app to import and process bank statements, which will help in updating the reconciliation status.

  2. Configuration for Checks/Effects:

    For checks, configure the system to update the invoice status only after the final bank settlement or encashment step. This involves using the ‘Reconciliation of Cashed Checks in Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable’ Reconciliation of Cashed Checks in Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable process, where checks are reconciled only after they are cashed. This ensures that the invoice is not immediately eligible for VAT declaration after the supplier payment posting but only after the check is encashed.

  3. Defining Triggering Events:

    It is possible to define the triggering event based on bank reconciliation or final clearing status by using the appropriate configuration settings in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. You can use the ‘Define Bank Clearing Account for Encashment of Outgoing Checks’ and ‘Define Document Type for Check Encashment’ configuration activities to manage this.

  4. Recommended CDS Views and Indicators:

Use standard CDS views that provide information on payment statuses and clearing indicators. These views can be used to track the status of payments and determine when an invoice should be eligible for VAT declaration. The ‘Clear Open Items Automatically’ Clearing Process app can be used to manage the clearing process and update the status of invoices accordingly.

By configuring these processes, you can ensure that supplier invoices are only eligible for VAT declaration after the appropriate financial events have occurred, such as bank reconciliation or check encashment.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: recreate or duplicate the closed activity using a Business Rule in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hello @everyone 

We are observing unexpected behavior when managing order statuses between SAP and FSM.

An order is initially set to TECO status in SAP, and the corresponding order is closed in FSM.
When we manually change the order status in SAP from TECO back to RELEASED and re‑execute the order:

  • The service call status is correctly updated to RELEASED
  • However, at the activity level, the status remains CLOSED

Could you please confirm whether this behavior is expected by design?
If so, can you explain why changing the order status from TECO to RELEASED at the order level does not reopen or update the activity status in FSM?
and if it's possible to recreate or duplicate the closed activity using a Business Rule in SAP FSM?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content: Hi Andrea. Did the repair fix your issue? you could tell by some changes in errors log as well as packagestemp folder will have 0-4/5 files (which higher versions already exist). regarding the CMS request port - It could be possible - but only if 6450 is blocked. Another thing is, when installer starts temp cms, its using java script to build the own commandline (not through the SIA wrapper) - which means, any setting you have in CMS DB do apply to temp CMS. The installer command lines are pretty much hardcoded to default which means it may already be using unique ports. The only way to tell what happened to the CMS is to look for CMS* logs at the same time addnode log (or deployfiles) logs get created and see if cms is running, and if it was accepting requests or not.

entry.title: Re: recreate or duplicate the closed activity using a Bus...
entry.content:
Hello Aziz,

Thank you for reaching out and providing a clear description of the issue. Please find our questions below.

1. Is This Behavior Expected?

Yes. When an order reverts from TECO to RELEASED in SAP:

  • Service Call → Correctly updates to RELEASED.
  • Activity → Remains CLOSED by design.

Activities represent completed execution work (labor, materials, confirmations). The system intentionally does not reopen them to preserve data integrity and prevent unintended rework or re-dispatching.

  • See also SAP Help Portal about 'Service Call Statuses and Mapping'. 

2. Why Doesn’t the Activity Reopen?

The activity lifecycle is decoupled from the order/service call lifecycle. Closed activities are treated as finalized operational records — reopening them could compromise confirmed efforts, signatures, and audit trails.

  • See also SAP Help Portal about 'Activity Statuses'.

3. Can a Business Rule Create a New Activity?

Yes. You can configure a Business Rule in FSM:

  • Trigger: Service Call status changes to RELEASED.
  • Condition: Existing activity status = CLOSED.
  • Action: Create a new activity on the same service call, carrying over relevant fields (address, equipment, skills) while excluding completed data.

Business Rule Logic (Example)

Below is a suggested structure for the Business Rule configuration:

Parameter Value

ObjectService Call
EventOn Update
Condition 1Service Call Status = RELEASED
Condition 2Activity Status (linked) = CLOSED
ActionCreate New Activity
Fields to CopyAddress, Equipment, Required Skills, Contact, Business Partner
Fields to ExcludeEffort Records, ChecklistInstanceElements (responses), Signatures, Timestamps

Implementation Steps:

  1. Navigate to Administration → Business Rules in FSM.
  2. Create a new rule with the trigger "Service Call – On Update."
  3. Define conditions to check that the status changed to RELEASED and that no open activity already exists (to prevent duplicates).
  4. Set the action to create a new activity with the relevant mapped fields.

Ensure you test in a sandbox environment to avoid duplicate creation scenarios.

  • See also SAP Help Portal about 'Busines Rules'.

entry.title: Unable to Post TRM Flows Due to Missing Payment Blocking Indicator for Customer Line Items
entry.content:
Hi.

We are currently implementing an internal loan process in the Treasury Management module in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

For this scenario, a financial transaction was created successfully and settled using the settlement function. In the payment conditions, under the control section, the option “Posting to Customer” was selected.

However, during the “Post Flows” step, the posting cannot be completed because the system requests a payment blocking indicator for the customer line items related to the generated payment request.

In the on-premise version of SAP, we found a related configuration setting for this issue. However, we could not find an equivalent configuration activity or SSCUI in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Could you please advise whether there is an equivalent configuration in Public Cloud or what the recommended approach/workaround would be to resolve this issue?

We have attached screenshots of the error message, the related transaction settings, and the corresponding on-premise configuration for reference.

Thank you for your support.

 

Best regards,

Wolfgang

My BOM components have been modified with an ECN, and the parameters I passed are correct. Has anyone encountered this situation before?

  LOOP AT lt_line ASSIGNING <ls_line>.
    CLEAR:lv_datuv,lt_idnrk[].
    lv_datuv = <ls_line>-datuv.
    CALL FUNCTION 'CSAP_MAT_BOM_READ'
      EXPORTING
        material    = <ls_line>-matnr
        plant       = <ls_line>-werks
        bom_usage   = '1'
        alternative = <ls_line>-stlal
        valid_from  = lv_datuv
      TABLES
        t_stpo      = lt_idnrk
      EXCEPTIONS
        error       = 1
        OTHERS      = 2.
    CLEAR:lv_idnrk.
    lv_idnrk = <ls_line>-idnrk.
    lv_idnrk = |{ lv_idnrk ALPHA = OUT }|.
    LOOP AT lt_idnrk ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_idnrk>) WHERE COMPONENT = lv_idnrk AND ITEM_NO = <ls_line>-posnr.

      ls_comp-maintain_mode        = 'C'.
      ls_comp-flag_bar_pointer = '0000000001'.
      ls_comp-sequence_no          = '000000'.
      ls_comp-activity             = <ls_line>-vornr.

      ls_comp-bom_type             = 'M'.
      ls_comp-bom_type_root        = 'M'.
      ls_comp-bom_no               = <ls_idnrk>-BOM_NO.
      ls_comp-bom_no_root          = <ls_idnrk>-BOM_NO.
      ls_comp-alternative_bom      = <ls_line>-stlal.
      ls_comp-alternative_bom_root = <ls_line>-stlal.
      ls_comp-item_id              = <ls_idnrk>-ITEM_NODE.
      ls_comp-item_no              = <ls_idnrk>-ITEM_no.
      ls_comp-plant                = <ls_line>-werks.
      ls_comp-material             = <ls_line>-matnr.
      READ TABLE lt_marc TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS WITH KEY idnrk = <ls_line>-idnrk BINARY SEARCH.
      IF sy-subrc = 0 OR <ls_idnrk>-spproctype = '50'.
         ls_comp-order_lvl = '01'.
         ls_comp-order_path = '01'.
         ls_compx-order_lvl = 'X'.
         ls_compx-order_path = 'X'.
      ENDIF.
      ls_compx-sequence_no          = 'X'.
      ls_compx-activity             = 'X'.
      ls_compx-plant                = 'X'.
      ls_compx-material             = 'X'.
      ls_compx-alternative_bom_root = 'X'.
      ls_compx-bom_type_root        = 'X'.
      ls_compx-bom_no_root          = 'X'.

      APPEND ls_comp TO lt_comp.
      APPEND ls_compx TO lt_compx.
      CLEAR:ls_comp,ls_compx.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDLOOP.

*  DATA:LV_MODE TYPE C VALUE 'C'.
  CALL FUNCTION 'CPCC_S_TASK_LIST_MAINTAIN'
    EXPORTING
      key_date                = lv_key_date
      task_list_type          = 'N'
      task_list_group         = lv_task_list_group
*     TASK_MAINTAIN_MODE      = LV_MODE
      group_counter           = lv_group_counter
      application             = 'PP01'
      bom_usage               = lv_bom_usage
      material                = lv_material  
      plant                   = lv_plant  
    TABLES
      component_allocations   = lt_comp
      component_allocations_x = lt_compx
      return                  = lt_return.

entry.title: Re: Problem connecting with ODBC
entry.content: Compile and see if that is the only "error". You can then compile with ASAN_OPTIONS=alloc_dealloc_mismatch=0. Other option is to turn password encryption off from the ODBC driver side to validate and test if the ASE isn't setup with forced password encryption ("sp_configure 'net password'" value back is 0). ODBC connection string/DSN EncryptPassword=0

entry.title: Manage Maintenance Orders Cost Visibility
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am experiencing an issue with the SAP Fiori application Manage Maintenance Orders(F5241) regarding cost visibility. 

In ıw32 , planned and actual costs on a maintenance order are populated correctly based on the components (materials) entered in the order. 

However, when accessing the same maintenance order through the  'Manage Maintenance Orders' app, the costs section displays the following message: "There are no costs with the selected G/L account hierarchy.''

The application requires a **G/L Account Hierarchy** to be selected along with a ledger and currency, but no default hierarchy is being proposed, and the cost data does not populate. Additionally, currency and ledger fields appear to be inactive.

As far as I understand, the G/L Account Hierarchy configuration is missing; otherwise, could the issue be caused by something?  Is there a way to set a **default G/L account hierarchy** for the Fiori app? In settings can set it actually if its true.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ME51N Document Number at Creation
entry.content:
There is no tutorial specifically for this.

var message = session.findById("wnd[0]/sbar").text;

will get the current message text from the status bar. The method setMessage of sbar will set it to whatever you want. For example,

session.findById("wnd[0]/sbar").setMessage("Hello", "S");

will display the success message "Hello".

In your script, try to get the current success message before switching to the custom tab, and once the tab switch happened, display the message you grabbed earlier.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: A Trusted Foundation for Enterprise-Ready Agentic AI
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite: A Trusted Foundation for Enterprise-Ready Agentic AI

AI agents are rapidly moving from market momentum to business priority. For enterprises, the opportunity is not just to build smarter agents, but to put them to work across applications, data, and processes in ways that are connected, secure, and ready for enterprise use.

That is where SAP Integration Suite comes in.

SAP Integration Suite is SAP's comprehensive integration offering that helps enterprises turn AI potential into business value by providing the trusted connectivity, built-in governance, and AI-assisted integration capabilities needed to operationalize AI agents across the enterprise.

 

This comes to life across three key areas of benefits: connectivity, governance, and accelerated productivity.

Connect AI agents to the flow of the business

For AI agents to create real business value, not only do they need model access, they need secure access to the systems, processes, and data that drive the business.

SAP Integration Suite helps extend trusted enterprise integration to AI agents across data, processes, and applications, making it easier to move from pilots to production in three important ways:

  • With support for emerging protocols such as MCP, enterprises can build on their existing API management investments by turning enterprise APIs into governed tools for agent use. This helps customers unlock more value from their investments in APIs, while enabling AI agents to interact with business systems in a more secure and scalable way.
  • Thousands of prebuilt, maintained integration assets, accessible through Joule, SAP's AI copilot embedded across SAP applications and technology, help accelerate agent connectivity across SAP and third-party landscapes. This reduces implementation effort, shortens time to value, and makes it easier to bring agent-driven scenarios into production across complex enterprise environments.
  • Event-driven integration and access to real-time data help agents respond to what is happening now, so they can support relevant, responsive, and business-aware execution.

Connecting AI agents is only part of the equation. To scale with confidence, enterprises also need to govern those interactions with the enterprise-grade controls, visibility, and trust.

Govern design-time and runtime with enterprise confidence for AI agents

As AI agents become more embedded in business processes, governance becomes an essential part of enterprise readiness.

Enterprises need clear control over how agents interact with enterprise systems, including what they can access, what actions they can take, and how those interactions are secured, monitored, and governed. SAP Integration Suite provides trusted design-time and runtime governance so businesses can scale agent adoption with greater confidence. This includes:

  • Govern MCP-based agent access with greater control by exposing curated enterprise APIs as governed tools that agents can securely discover and invoke across SAP and third-party systems, extending trusted API governance and policy control into agent interactions.
  • Manage MCP server discovery and access more deliberately by surfacing verified MCP servers in Developer Hub with trusted identity and authentication, helping enterprises reduce risk and give teams a more controlled path to adoption.
  • Enable more secure, cost-aware, and context-rich AI interactions by bringing together native AI usage management and multi-LLM connectivity across SAP and third-party systems.
  • Strengthen operational trust at scale with observability into MCP executions and runtime guardrails such as Traffic Prediction and Anomaly Detection, helping enterprises identify unusual patterns earlier, anticipate traffic more proactively, and maintain stable agent execution.

This helps enterprises move beyond experimentation and empower agent-driven scenarios with the control, transparency, and confidence required for enterprise use. But connectivity and governance are only a fraction of the value. Enterprises also need a faster and more efficient way to build, run, and scale agent-driven integration scenarios by making agentic integration itself more autonomous.

Accelerate agent connectivity with AI-assistance throughout the integration lifecycle

As demand grows for intelligent, connected business processes, speed and efficiency become increasingly important.

Teams need to reduce manual effort, simplify complex integration work, and bring new agent-driven scenarios to life faster. SAP Integration Suite supports this with AI-assisted capabilities across the integration lifecycle, helping enterprises improve productivity while maintaining the reliability, control, and standards required in the enterprise. This includes capabilities that help teams:

  • Speed up integration design with Joule-assisted integration flow generation
  • Support complex B2B scenarios with AI-assisted message transformation
  • Surface API insights faster with conversational analytics
  • Improve operational efficiency with AI-assisted optimization, troubleshooting, and documentation

The result is faster delivery, more efficient handling of complexity, and stronger operational excellence, helping enterprises realize value more quickly from agent-driven integration scenarios.

When connectivity, governance, and AI-assisted acceleration come together, agentic AI becomes far more than a technology promise. It becomes a practical way to connect intelligence to real business operations, with the context, control, and scalability enterprises need.

SAP Integration Suite helps enterprises make that shift by connecting AI agents to the systems, data, and processes they rely on, governing those interactions with enterprise-grade controls, and accelerating integration with AI-assisted capabilities across the lifecycle. For businesses looking to turn AI agents into real operational value, integration is not peripheral to the story. It is the foundation. 

 

Next Steps

Explore the SAP Integration Suite for agentic AI demo to see how trusted connectivity, runtime governance, and accelerated integration enable a real enterprise agent-driven scenario. You can also learn more about how SAP Integration Suite can support agentic AI here.

entry.title: Re: Bin Blocked by inventory active
entry.content:
Try using LI20 tcode.  I had similar issue after a cycle count on storage types not setup for cycle count.  Each bin that a count was done on in one of those storage types had the IA block.  I went to LI20, selected the ones with the block and pressed the Write Off button.
Worked for me.  Don't know if that will help you or not.

entry.title: Re: Seeking Guidance on SAP Business Suite Testing Learni...
entry.content:
Hi @MikeCuevas 

Thank you for reaching out—and great to see your interest in transitioning into SAP testing. With your strong QA background and extensive experience across different testing types, you already have a solid foundation to build on.

To help you get started, I would suggest the following approach:
Kickstart your learning, build a strong foundation with introductory courses
Begin with an overview of SAP and its core concepts. Getting familiar with SAP S/4HANA and how business processes are structured will make it much easier to understand testing scenarios later on.

Boost your expertise at your own pace
On learning.sap.com, you’ll find structured learning journeys that guide you step by step. While there isn’t always a dedicated “SAP Tester” path, journeys related to S/4HANA, Business Process Integration, or specific modules are highly relevant.

As a tester, understanding business processes is critical. I recommend gaining basic knowledge in commonly used modules such as. This will help you design and execute more meaningful end-to-end test scenarios.

Achieve SAP Certification: Consider starting with a foundational certification. 
If possible, try to get access to practice systems or hands-on exercises.

I hope this helps as a starting point, and please feel free to reach out if you’d like more specific recommendations.

Best regards,
Margit

entry.title: Re: Generate 1 cheque from multiple payment document with...
entry.content:
Dear @PAAA,

In Public Cloud one cheque for several supplier invoices is possible if the invoices are grouped into one payment during the automatic payment run. The cheque output follows the payment result so the key requirement is that the payment run creates one consolidated payment / payment document for the supplier, not several separate payment documents. The standard app for this process is Manage Automatic Payments which SAP describes as the app used to schedule payment proposals/payments and monitor proposal or payment status

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/45698054f87c033de10000000a441470.html

To achieve this, you should verify the payment grouping settings before the payment proposal is created:

  • In the supplier master data, the supplier must not be marked for Individual Payment / Single Payment. SAP confirms this check in the context of payment consolidation: in Maintain Business Partner Supplier Payment Transactions the Single Payment option should be unchecked when multiple lines are expected to be selected and paid together

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3704816

  • In SSCUI 101044 Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions the cheque payment method must not be configured to force individual payments, for example via Single Payment for Marked Item, Payment per Due Day, or Extended Individual Payment. At process level SAP explains that the payment run groups due items for a business partner into payable groups, provided the relevant grouping conditions are fulfilled

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/280bc5536a51204be10000000a174cb4.html

  • SAP also confirms that Single Payment for marked item is maintained in configuration Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions / ID 101044

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3504757

  • In SSCUI 101001 Set Up Paying Company Codes for Payment Transactions, check whether Separate Payment for each Ref. is active. SAP confirms that if this indicator is used, only invoices and credit memos with the same payment reference can be settled with one payment

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3198883

Summary:

Yes the requirement is supported, but it is controlled by payment grouping in the automatic payment run. The correct solution is to prevent the system from splitting the supplier’s invoices into separate payment documents before the cheque is generated

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Alternative G/L Account description not displayed in...
entry.content:
The behavior is caused by language-dependent master data texts.

The Alternative G/L Account description displayed in analytical reporting applications is retrieved according to the user’s logon language. If no text is maintained for a specific Alternative G/L Account in that language, the description will not be displayed in the report.

This can appear inconsistent because:

Some Alternative G/L Accounts may already have texts maintained in multiple languages
Other accounts may only have texts maintained in a single language

As a result, descriptions are shown only for accounts where a text exists in the current logon language.

Recommended validation:

Log on using a language in which the Alternative G/L Account description is maintained.
Re-run the affected report.
Verify whether the description is displayed.

If the description appears in that language, the behavior is confirmed to be working as designed.

To ensure consistent display across users and reports, maintain the Alternative G/L Account descriptions in all required logon languages.

entry.title: iRPA - SAP Build Process Automation: cannot fetch automation job logs and output via Public API
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm triggering an automation in SAP Build Process Automation through an API Trigger, using the endpoint:

POST /apiTriggers/{triggerId}/runs

This works correctly and returns a jobId. I would now like to fetch the logs and output stored against that job via the API.

The endpoint GET /apiTriggers/{triggerId}/runs/{jobId} only returns the job's status and timestamps — it does not include the log entries or the output values that are visible in Monitoring → Automation Jobs in the Build cockpit.

The API documentation isn't clear on how to retrieve this additional data. Could you confirm:

  1. Is it possible to fetch the logs of a job through the Public API, and if so, which endpoint should be used?
  2. Is it possible to fetch the output / result payload of a finished job, and via which endpoint?
  3. Are any specific scopes or role collections required beyond those used to trigger the run?

Any pointers to the relevant API documentation would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance.

Dinesh

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q1-Q2 2026 Updates & Roadmap Highlights
entry.content:
Introduction

ESG reporting software is moving from an emerging category to a mission-critical business capability, driven by regulation, finance-led operating models, and a clear shift in buyer expectations. Even as regulatory scope evolves, demand remains strong: organizations still need to maintain or expand sustainability reporting, strengthen data integrity, and prepare for recurring disclosure requirements.

At the same time, manual and fragmented reporting models are no longer sustainable. Sustainability teams continue to face growing time burdens, computational complexity, and error risk when ESG data is collected across spreadsheets and disconnected processes. This is increasingly a finance-led concern as well: CFOs and finance leaders are becoming more influential in ESG funding decisions and are placing greater emphasis on the integrity of non-financial data.

AI is raising expectations even further. Business leaders increasingly expect reporting processes to become faster, more automated, and more intelligent. Still, sustainability AI can only create trusted business value when it is grounded in governed, traceable, enterprise-grade ESG data. Without this foundation, AI-assisted reporting can accelerate drafting, but it cannot reliably explain, evidence, or defend the data behind the disclosure.

This is where SAP Business AI becomes central to SAP Sustainability Control Tower. SAP’s approach is not AI added on top of disconnected sustainability data. It is AI embedded into business processes, supported by enterprise context, governed workflows, and traceable data from SAP and non-SAP systems. SAP Sustainability Control Tower is designed to support this journey by centralizing sustainability data and insights, helping organizations ensure compliant reporting, embed sustainability into core processes, and drive measurable progress across the enterprise.

These latest updates build on that foundation. They strengthen the data, integration, workflow, analytics, and reporting capabilities that make AI-assisted sustainability management credible in practice.

Here’s what’s new in SAP Sustainability Control Tower as of Q2 2026:

Expanded coverage of pre-delivered regulatory scope

To help organizations accelerate their sustainability reporting readiness, SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to expand its SAP-provided ESG reporting content. These enhancements support customers by providing additional value for ESRS metric coverage across existing SAP-owned and custom metrics.

Recent master data enhancements include new and enhanced dimensions, measures, and data points. For dimensions, new and enhanced content includes gender attributes, human rights incident values, grievance mechanism recipient types, and severity of human rights incidents. For measures, new content includes human rights incidents, monetary sanctions due to human rights incidents, and enhanced naming for the count of governance body members.

SAP-provided metric coverage for ESRS data points has also been significantly expanded. As of April 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower includes 200+ quantitative metrics and 650+ qualitative metrics, supporting organizations with a broader set of content for ESRS data-point requirements. This includes quantitative coverage across areas such as climate change, pollution, water and marine resources, biodiversity and ecosystems, resource use and circular economy, own workforce, and business conduct. Qualitative coverage spans ESRS 2 general disclosures and topical standards across environmental, social, and governance topics. Documentation is available in Help for SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

That growing catalog gives customers a stronger starting point, while still leaving room to extend the model for company-specific or industry-specific reporting needs.

Data collection process enhancements

Governed ESG reporting depends on clear ownership, timing, and workflow. SAP Sustainability Control Tower supports form-based data collection for scoped measures and metrics, helping organizations define how data should be collected, when it should be collected, and who should provide and approve the submitted information.

With the data collection process, users can define data collection forms and set up data collection instances to trigger workflow-based data collection for selected items. They can assign processors for a given period and define approvers to review submitted data for approval or rejection.

Recent enhancements extend support to qualitative metrics in form-based data collection. Users can group qualitative metrics within a single form, and when new fiscal variants are activated, existing forms linked to inactive fiscal variants are automatically deactivated. In addition, table headers remain visible while scrolling, making larger data collection forms easier to use.

The qualitative metric data entry experience has also been redesigned. Users can select from all available metrics and add data at the high-level corporate summary level or at a more granular reporting structure level. Relevant details are available in the reference section on the data entry screen, helping users understand the context for the information they are entering.

The reporting line is now also available as an organizational boundary in data collection. Users can create a reporting scope using the reporting line organization type when setting up organizational boundaries, then run “check for updates” in the Configure Data Collection or Configure Data Entry app to add the new entries to the configurable items list.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also enhances integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data. Customers can import GHG emissions data from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower while preserving native source granularity.

The integration helps eliminate the need for external data transformation and aggregation. Period type can be configured per integration scenario, with supported period types including monthly, quarterly, and yearly based on the active fiscal year variant. Data is ingested into SAP Sustainability Control Tower at the selected granularity, without forced transformation, and replicated to target systems using the configured period type.

Recent enhancements include a new UI control for period granularity selection and configurable period selection, including single-period and period-range options. Fiscal year variant support also helps enable more accurate period mapping.

The same principle of flexibility applies to the broader ESG data model, where customers often need to adapt standard content to their own structures and reporting practices.

ESG Data Foundation extensibility

As sustainability reporting requirements evolve, organizations need flexibility to adapt data models, extend content, and transport configuration across environments. SAP Sustainability Control Tower provides an open, extensible ESG Data Foundation that supports both standard SAP content and customer-specific extensions.

Customers can add custom measures across Data Provider Interfaces and add up to 20 custom dimensions for SAP-provided or custom measures. The content lifecycle for extension artifacts supports custom measures, dimensions, and metrics, with export and import via JSON file format.

Recent enhancements improve the content lifecycle for extension artifacts. Export and import are available in the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. Export selection can be based on naming conventions and includes dependent custom measures and custom dimensions to help ensure consistency.

Import supports consistency in the target tenant during object creation, and can now also update existing extensibility artifacts.

This makes extension management more practical across systems and tenants, especially as ESG reporting models continue to evolve.

SAP Datasphere content coverage

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also keeps enhancing its integration with SAP Datasphere. Users can leverage SAP Datasphere integration to load actual data into the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app and to upload master data for reporting structures, such as business locations, legal entities, and reporting lines, into the Manage Master Data app.

The SAP Datasphere content package supports multiple Data Provider Interfaces, including accounting, corruption incidents, emissions, generic measures, general measures, injury, land site, people, resource use, water withdrawal, waste, energy, and removal reversal carbon credit. Recent enhancements include updates for emissions and people DPIs, as well as new content for removal reversal and carbon credit scenarios.

The next step is to make those connected data flows easier to apply in regulation-specific scenarios, such as the EU Taxonomy.

EU Taxonomy data integration enhancements

For EU Taxonomy reporting, SAP Sustainability Control Tower now offers enhanced capabilities to manage master data and measures data in a more integrated way.

In the Manage Master Data app, users can configure and manage master data and EU Taxonomy filters in one place. This includes managing company code values used by EU Taxonomy in the legal entity dimension, defining filters applied to SAP S/4HANA master data and activity data, importing master data and hierarchies from files, and importing master data and hierarchies from SAP S/4HANA with the SAP Data Framework.

 

In the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app, users can import EU Taxonomy measures data, including activity data and denominator data. The integration supports imports from files and SAP S/4HANA, with validation rules to check consistency against master data and filters defined in the Manage Master Data app.

This simplifies how EU Taxonomy data is prepared, imported, validated, and exposed to downstream processes, while reducing the need for separate replication across the Manage Master Data app, the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app, and CPE-UM processes.

Analyze ESG Data app enhancements

SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to evolve the Analyze ESG Data app from a reporting experience into a steering experience. The app helps users explore ESG performance with more flexible analysis, personalization, and target comparison capabilities.

Recent updates introduce target-versus-actual visualization in charts for selected metrics, periods, and reporting structures. This enables immediate performance comparison without additional configuration.

 

Users can also benefit from a flexible chart-specific configuration model that allows independent control over metric selection, filters, drilldowns, chart types, reusable public or private configurations, default views, and embedded descriptions for context.

The multi-chart analytics experience has also been enhanced. Users can display up to six charts in parallel, combine chart and table views, and adjust drilldowns directly in charts. This supports faster exploration and side-by-side analysis across different sustainability performance perspectives.

Generate ESG Reports with AI enhancements

The Generate ESG Reports with AI app matures as a way to reduce manual effort while giving users more control over ESG report creation. Users can generate ESG reports using prebuilt templates and AI-assisted drafting to support timely, accurate, and consistent reporting.

When generating a report, users can preview or download the result as a PDF. For added customization, users can manually edit sections, and reports are now available in DOCX format.

SAP’s built-in AI assistance supports dynamic revisions. Users can select a passage and use the Revise option to receive suggestions to shorten or lengthen the text, helping refine the final ESG narrative with greater ease and speed.

In addition, each report can be generated in one of 20 languages using AI-based translation. 

Planned features and roadmap highlights

Forward-looking statement: The following roadmap items are planned and may change.

Looking ahead to the remainder of 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower plans to continue strengthening regulatory coverage and embedded AI capabilities for audit-ready ESG reporting. 

Planned topics include further coverage of ESRS metrics, reporting scope management with inbound interfaces for materiality assessment results, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration for energy consumption, unit of measure management, evidence management for data collection, availability of sustainability metrics data product in SAP Business Data Cloud, integration with SAP Success Factors through SAP Business Data Cloud, validation rules, SOC 2 certification and user experience improvements for the overall reporting flow.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also plans to expand support for IFRS sustainability disclosure requirements. Planned IFRS content provisioning includes comprehensive IFRS coverage, compatible metric definitions that enhance the ESRS offering, and IFRS-specific metric definitions.

Moreover, SAP Sustainability Control Tower is designed to support interoperability across sustainability reporting standards.  Within metric general information, customers can see interoperability between disclosure requirements across standards such as ESRS and IFRS, helping organizations address multiple reporting frameworks through a single reporting effort.

 

SAP also continues to explore AI-enabled support for sustainability regulatory readiness. The Sustainability Regulatory Readiness Agent is one of the planned agents, designed to help accelerate governed, audit-ready sustainability reporting by automating the assembly of reporting scope based on materiality assessments, creating data points for new regulations, mapping existing metrics, and proposing custom metrics where coverage gaps exist. 

Stay tuned for more and check SCT Roadmap Explorer.

Additional information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, we recommend browsing the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

Stay tuned for more updates as SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to help organizations move from ESG data to actionable insights, compliant reporting, and measurable sustainability performance.

entry.title: Re: Manage Maintenance Orders Cost Visibility
entry.content:
Hi MuratTnrkt,

 

As you mentioned, IW32 is displaying both planned and actual costs correctly, which indicates that PM order costing itself is functioning as expected.

The discrepancy appears to be specific to the Fiori app Manage Maintenance Orders (F5241), which derives and displays costs differently based on Ledger and G/L Account Hierarchy, resulting in a variation from what is seen in IW32.

I recommend checking with the FI/CO configuration team to review the following:

  • G/L account hierarchy configuration
  • Hierarchy assignment and default settings
  • Relevant cost element / G/L accounts
  • Analytical authorizations and CDS view access used by Fiori, to ensure there are no authorization or data visibility issues

Additionally, inactive ledger and currency fields are often default settings in S/4HANA, which can further influence how costs are presented in analytically driven Fiori apps.

Overall, this appears to be a Finance/Fiori configuration issue rather than a PM order costing issue.

For reference see the following SAP Community article for more information on managing hierarchies in SAP S/4HANA: Manage your hierarchies in FIORI 1/3 - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Best Practices Recommendations for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Tomáš 

thank you so much for sharing your experience. What you say about designing spaces is exactly what I wanted to get across: "use as few spaces as possible to still effectively separate authorization - just as many as needed to do so."  If there is a use case for separating authorizations, like for securing HR topic or FI topic, a dedicated space should be set up, but the overall message should be: Try to keep the number of spaces small, to avoid an overhead of administrative tasks and shared entities. Of course, it is like with any authorization concept: Try to keep it simple, but allow the required limits and flexibility.

However, there is one further aspect that directs us towards a smaller number of spaces.  Within a space with many objects, you can group models into folders and sub-folders to separate topics and modelling layers. In contrast, if you create separate spaces, you need to share many objects, and these shared objects appear on the main level of the target space and can't easily be organized within the target space.

 

Regarding Virtualization versus Replication, it is really a matter of balancing the pro and con. Yes, Datasphere offers cheaper ways to store data sets, but so does BW if you use Near-line storage or Data Tiering Optimization. The main questions to decide this question are: 

* Do you need totally up-to date values (then go for virtual models) or is some latency acceptable (then replicate the data)?

* How often (and with which performance expectations) are the dataset analyzed repeatedly? (Replication is most important for frequently needed data.)

* How large is the replicated data volume? (Accessing large data sets across systems is rather slow, so the need to optimize performance is higher, but the storage will be more expensive, too.)

* Should we rather replicate values to reduce the stress on the source system? (Yes, try to extract only once instead of frequently accessing the data. But you don't want to replicate a data segment that is hardly ever needed.)

 

Persisting views (on final level before the Analytic Model) is a good idea to improve performance for a specific purpose, but it generates additional tasks or confusion when the view is changed. So, I recommend it mainly for short-term storage or for stable models.

I am looking forward to more and other opinions, experience, and discussion.

Thanks again for sharing your thoughts.

 Alexander

entry.title: Connecting SAP S/4HANA with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh via Proxy Configuration
entry.content:
This blog post intends to complement the existing information on the following blog posts:

SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh

SAP S/4HANA and SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) direct integration

 

Whenever a proxy server is configured for SAP S/4HANA outbound traffic, certain configuration changes must be made, while other changes must be requested through a Service Request.

If your customer landscape uses a proxy server for outbound traffic that must be maintained in the SM59 destination, and you receive the following error when activating the AEM channel, this means that your RFC destination of type G does not support TCPS connectivity through a proxy server. In this case, the procedure for routing outbound traffic for your Event channels will be different. One possible solution is to implement a load balancer to route events from SAP S/4HANA, while using the proxy configuration only for the authentication service.

 

 

Requesting a Hyperscaler Load Balancer for Outbound Traffic

The TCPS protocol is required for the AMQP connectivity configured in the Event Channel. So, a solution is to establish this connection directly using a Hyperscaler Load Balancer. 

For that open a Service Request using the template Create Hyperscaler LB for OUTBOUND traffic to an EXTERNAL source(Azure/AWS/GCP).

 

Select the SAP S/4HANA system that needs to be connected with the AEM broker and insert the requested parameters:

Customer ID - the SAP S/4HANA system that needs to be connected.

Select Hyperscaler - the hyperscaler where the selected SAP S/4HANA system is hosted.

Primary or DR Site - if the service is to be performed on the Primary or the DR site.

Source IP Addresses - the IP address of the selected SAP S/4HANA system.

Destination IP Addresses - the Solace broker instance IP address or the host specified with protocol prefix: tcps://<hostname>

Destination Ports - 5671 (port used for AMQP connectivity)

 

Confirm and schedule service.

 

Updating the OAuth profile in SAP S/4HANA

You have created a AEM channel using a service key and the activation fails with the following error:

You have checked the OAuth configuration using the report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS, as described in KBA 3444844, and you are receiving the following error:

>> lcl_oa2c_app->EXECUTE_ATR
granttype Client Credentials configured.

HTTP failure, processing failed, invalid state, invalid timeout or others
oa2c_error
Client configuration error or network problems. See kernel traces.

 

Run transaction OA2C_CONFIG:

1. Select the Configuration Name created by your channel.

2. Under tab Administration - Access Settings - Add the values for proxy host and proxy port:

Save the OAuth2 Profile.

 

Run transaction SM59:

1. Select the type G destination created by your channel for the BTP AEM Validation Service.

2. Under Technical Settings - HTTP Proxy Options - Add the values for Proxy Host and Proxy Service:

Save and close.

 

3. Select the type G destination that you created for the Solace broker.

4. Under Technical Settings - Target System Settings - Maintain the same Host and Port used in Destination IP Addresses and Destination Ports requested in the SR Template Create Hyperscaler LB for OUTBOUND traffic to an EXTERNAL source(Azure/AWS/GCP).

 

5. Under Technical Settings - Target System Settings - HTTP Proxy Options - Remove the values for Proxy Host and Service:

Save and close.

 

Go to transaction  SE38 and execute the report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS again. Then confirm that you receive the following message:

 

 

Activating the AEM Channel in SAP S/4HANA

Run transaction IWXBE/CONFIG and activate your channel. Once the channel has been activated, perform a connectivity test.

If your channel activation fails, go to transaction SMICM and check the error in the Trace File.

entry.title: Entering the New Era of Agentic AI for ABAP Development
entry.content:
By Sonja Liénard, Head of ABAP Platform, SAP SE 

At SAP Sapphire 2026, we made a set of announcements that together mark the most significant step forward in ABAP AI since we first introduced SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities. Across tooling, agentic capabilities, platform reach, and commercial model, these announcements reflect a clear direction: making ABAP development smarter and more accessible to every customer — regardless of where they are in their SAP journey. 

Key highlights 

  • ABAP enters the era of agentic AI with the general availability of the ABAP MCP Server — open to your choice of agentic assistants, orchestrators, and LLMs  
  • ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code will be generally available in Q2 2026, completing our commitment to your choice of IDE for ABAP developers 
  • The first ABAP custom code agent for SAP S/4HANA migration releasing in Q2 2026 
  • ABAP AI available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, all releases from 2021, in Q2 2026 
  • A new consumption-based commercial model for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities — transparent, predictable, and well-suited for agentic AI workloads 

 

ABAP Enters the Era of Agentic AI 

At SAP TechEd 2025, we announced our intention to bring agentic AI to ABAP development. At Sapphire 2026, we shared that this is becoming generally available in Q2 2026. 

The foundation for this is the ABAP Language Server and the ABAP MCP Server built on top of it. The ABAP Language Server provides the abstraction layer that makes ABAP development capabilities available independently of the IDE.  

The ABAP MCP Server builds on this foundation using Model Context Protocol (MCP) — an emerging open standard that allows AI agents to interact with external tools and systems in a structured and reliable way. This opens ABAP development to the full breadth of the agentic AI ecosystem: coding agents such as GitHub Copilot or Amazon Q can now perform complex ABAP coding tasks directly within the developer's IDE. The MCP Server is available in both Eclipse and VS Code. 

The first ABAP agent built on this foundation — supporting custom code migration from ECC to SAP S/4HANA — becomes available in Q2 2026. 

An AI agent actively remediating ABAP findings in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse in real time 

 

ABAP Development Tools for VS Code 

ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will be generally available in Q2 2026. 

This is the living proof of our commitment to your IDE choice that we have been building toward for some time. VS Code is one of the world's most widely used IDEs, with a rich ecosystem of AI extensions. Building on the ABAP Language Server and ABAP MCP Server as the technical foundation, ADT for VS Code supports an agentic developer experience and gives ABAP developers direct access to modern coding agents like GitHub Copilot.  

The initial scope focuses on SAP Fiori app development, with additional object types to follow.  

Eclipse remains the IDE for full-fledged ABAP development until we reach feature parity with VS Code. Developers can choose between Eclipse and VS Code depending on their workflow and preference. 

Custom Code Agents for SAP S/4HANA Migration 

SAP S/4HANA migration projects carry significant complexity, and a large part of that complexity lies in custom ABAP code — business logic built up over many years, often undocumented and tightly coupled with SAP standard objects. Understanding it, adapting it, and validating it has historically been time consuming and expensive work.  

The Custom Code Assistant addresses this directly, bringing together a coherent, intelligent toolchain that covers all phases of an SAP S/4HANA custom code migration project. Three agents focusing on developer execution will be delivered by ABAP platform, complemented by additional agents from SAP Cloud ALM handling broader transformation governance. 

Custom Code Assistant with Custom Code Migration Agents 

The first CCM agent becomes available in Q2 2026 — the Mass S/4 Custom Code Conversion Agent, which identifies ATC issues for custom code adaptation and fixes them at scale, using deterministic rules or AI-generated fixes.  

Two further agents are planned for later in 2026: 

  • Mass Clean Core Adoption Agent — identifies clean-core ATC issues, fixes them, or provides structured recommendations where manual review is needed. 
  • Web Dynpro to SAP Fiori App Modernization Agent — converts legacy code to modern ABAP Cloud code. 

A core design principle across these agents is to first explain and understand the existing code before proposing any changes, to protect the investment customers have made in their custom code. Based on early evaluations, we are targeting 40% efficiency gain in custom code transformation work.  

Meeting You Where You Are 

At Sapphire, we announced that SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for all releases from 2021 onwards, with general availability planned for Q2 2026. This removes a significant ABAP AI adoption barrier — customers on older releases gain the same AI-powered development experience as those on the latest release.  

This is made possible through a hub service running on SAP BTP, which combines authentication, license management, access to SAP BTP AI Core, and ABAP-specific business logic. The hub plugs directly into the developer's IDE, making it possible to offer ABAP AI capabilities largely independent of the ABAP backend release. 

A New Commercial Model  

ABAP AI capabilities are part of SAP Joule for Developers — SAP's AI offering designed specifically for the developer experience.  

As of Q2 2026, we are introducing a consumption-based pricing model for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities, built on AI units — transparent, predictable, and well-suited for the dynamic nature of agentic AI workloads. 

Activation is simple via SAP for Me, with users assigned through Cloud Identity Services. 

What This Means for ABAP Developers 

Taken together, these announcements tell a clear story: ABAP development is becoming more intelligent, more open, and more capable of tackling the hardest problems in large enterprise landscapes — at a pace and scale that was not possible before. 

The ABAP MCP Server opens ABAP to the full breadth of the AI agent ecosystem — your choice of orchestrators, LLMs, and IDEs. ADT for VS Code brings ABAP into the environment that developers already use. The Custom Code Migration Agents address the SAP S/4HANA migration challenge with purpose-built AI that understands the business logic and structure of enterprise systems. And the expanded platform support means these capabilities are available also to customers on older releases. 

This is about further development and collaboration — ABAP and developers remain a strong team. AI takes on more of the repetitive, time-consuming tasks, creating space for what creates real value: understanding the business, making good architectural decisions, and delivering solutions that last. 

Sonja Liénard is Head of ABAP Platform at SAP SE. She leads the unit responsible for ABAP platform, ABAP Cloud, ABAP AI, and ABAP IDE strategy.

entry.title: Re: Assigning fixed assets to functional location
entry.content: Assigning a fixed asset to a functional location is typically done in SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) / Asset Accounting (FI-AA) integration. There isn’t a direct “assignment” field in the asset master itself—instead, the link is maintained via the equipment master record, which connects both the asset and the functional location.
